suggested_cluster_label: Thin-film solid-state battery fabrication via sputter deposition
engineering_module_candidates: Multi-layer cell stack assembly on substrate
Sputter/plasma deposition process and target materials
Cell patterning and inter-stack isolation (e.g., grooves, current collectors)
rationale: Top terms (electrode, electrolyte, substrate, cathode, layer, cell, plasma, deposition, target, battery, energy storage) and titles like 'ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', 'STACK FOR AN ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', and 'METHOD OF MAKING A COMPONENT FOR A SOLID-STATE ELECTROCHEMICAL CELL' indicate a focus on solid-state electrochemical cells built as layered stacks. Abstracts repeatedly describe depositing first electrode, electrolyte, and second electrode layers on a substrate, with references to grooves between stacks and current collectors, supporting modules for electrode deposition, electrolyte deposition, stack assembly, and sputter/plasma-based processes. Confidence is high for deposition and stack modules; the 'target' term suggests sputter targets but specific target chemistries are not detailed in the provided evidence.

engineering_module_candidates: Airflow generator (air inlet to air outlet path)
Styling attachments (interchangeable, with curved/flat surfaces downstream of outlet)
rationale: Top terms ('haircare appliance', 'hair styling', 'attachment', 'air inlet', 'air outlet', 'airflow', 'diffuser') and abstracts consistently describe a haircare/styling appliance generating airflow from inlet to outlet, with attachments featuring curved/flat surfaces downstream of the outlet. Title terms also include 'diffuser' and 'attachment handheld', supporting the attachment and handheld housing modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Primary Fluid Flow Path / Internal Ducting
rationale: Representative titles are dominated by 'HAND HELD APPLIANCE' and abstracts explicitly describe a hairdryer-type device with a body, duct, fluid inlet, fluid outlet, and a primary fluid flow path. Top TF-IDF terms ('fluid flow path', 'fluid inlet', 'fluid outlet', 'duct', 'wall') support internal flow-path and housing modules. Title terms 'heater assembly', 'attachment', and 'cleaning appliance' justify a heater module and an attachment interface, though 'cleaning appliance' suggests the cluster may span beyond hairdryers, so confidence on appliance-specific sub-modules is moderate.

suggested_cluster_label: Sensor-driven smart vacuum cleaners and mobile cleaning robots
engineering_module_candidates: Controller with signal-processing and classification modules
rationale: Top terms (vacuum motor, sensor, controller, mobile robot, vacuum cleaner) and representative titles dominated by 'VACUUM CLEANER' point to vacuum-based cleaning products. Abstracts repeatedly describe a vacuum motor, motion/orientation sensors, diagnostic sensors, a cleaner head with agitator, and a controller that processes sensor signals against a surface-type model — supporting suction, sensing, head, and controller modules. The presence of 'mobile', 'mobile robot', and 'robotic' in title terms supports a mobile robot platform module. Terms like 'oral' and 'hairstyling' also appear but are minority signals and not reflected in the shown abstracts, so they are not used as module evidence (lower confidence for non-vacuum themes).

suggested_cluster_label: Vacuum Cleaner Head with Agitator/Brush Bar
engineering_module_candidates: Cleaner head housing and suction chamber
Agitator/brush bar assembly and drive mechanism
Suction inlet and airflow path to suction generator
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cleaner head', 'agitator', 'brush bar', 'suction', 'housing', 'chamber') and representative titles ('CLEANER HEAD FOR A VACUUM CLEANER') consistently point to the floor-engaging cleaner head sub-system. Abstracts describe a housing defining a suction chamber with inlet/outlet, rotatable agitators with drive mechanisms, and one mentions a laser diode for illuminating debris—supporting the listed modules. Other potential sub-modules (e.g., dirt separation hinted by 'dirt separator' in title terms) are only weakly evidenced here, so confidence on those is lower.

suggested_cluster_label: Surface treating appliance main body & support assembly architecture
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('main body', 'support assembly', 'hose', 'wand', 'surface treating head', 'appliance') and representative titles uniformly referencing 'Surface treating appliance' indicate this cluster centers on the overall mechanical architecture of vacuum-cleaner-type appliances. Abstracts explicitly describe a main body with a rollable support assembly, a hose/wand assembly, and a surface treating head, directly supporting the listed modules. Mentions of 'robotic' and 'domestic appliance' in title terms are minor and not strongly evidenced in abstracts, so they are not included as separate modules.

suggested_cluster_label: Multi-stage cyclonic dirt/dust separation for vacuum cleaners
engineering_module_candidates: Airflow path with dirty-air inlet and clean-air outlet
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cyclonic separating', 'cyclone', 'dirt', 'dust', 'separator', 'chamber', 'stage', 'vacuum cleaner') and titles like 'MULTISTAGE CYCLONIC SEPARATING APPARATUS' and 'Dirt and Dust Cyclonic Separating Apparatus' point to multi-stage cyclonic separation. Abstracts explicitly describe a first cyclonic unit with an annular dust collecting chamber and a downstream second cyclonic unit, supporting separate first/second stage and collection chamber modules. Repeated 'HANDHELD CLEANING APPLIANCE' titles with described dirty-air inlet, clean-air outlet, and separating apparatus support the airflow-path and handheld housing modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Nozzle airflow module (interior passage and mouth for emitting air)
Base airflow generation module (air inlet, impeller, motor)
rationale: Representative titles are dominated by 'FAN ASSEMBLY' and top title terms include 'nozzle fan' and 'humidifying'. Abstracts describe a bladeless fan with a base housing an impeller and motor driving air through a nozzle's interior passage and mouth, matching top TF-IDF terms ('nozzle', 'interior passage', 'mouth', 'impeller', 'air inlet/outlet'). Humidification is supported by the 'humidifying' title term, though only weakly represented in the shown abstracts, so that module is lower confidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Brushless (permanent-magnet) motor control via winding excitation
engineering_module_candidates: AC rectification and DC-link conditioning stage
Phase current sensing and threshold/timeout regulation
rationale: Titles overwhelmingly read 'CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS MOTOR' (and permanent-magnet variants), and top terms include 'winding', 'voltage', 'brushless motor', 'phase winding', 'current', and 'period'. Abstracts consistently describe exciting a motor winding with a (often rectified) voltage and freewheeling based on current thresholds or timeout/overrun periods, directly supporting drive-control, excitation/freewheel logic, rectification, and current-sensing modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Rotor assembly (shaft, rotor core, magnet, impeller)
Motor frame / housing (outer and support/inner portions)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (rotor, stator, bearing, shaft, frame, impeller, adhesive, stator core, rotor assembly) and titles dominated by 'BRUSHLESS MOTOR' and 'COMPRESSOR' indicate the cluster centers on electric motor electromechanical assemblies. Abstracts consistently describe a rotor assembly (shaft + rotor core + bearing assembly + impeller), a stator assembly (including C-shaped stator cores), and a frame with outer/support portions, supporting the listed modules. The recurring mention of 'impeller' and 'compressor' supports a turbomachine integration module, while 'adhesive' as a distinctive term supports an adhesive-fixation aspect. Confidence is high for motor/bearing/frame modules; compressor integration is supported but appears in a subset of the evidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Cleaning appliances: dental fluid-jet devices and vacuum floor tools
engineering_module_candidates: Handle assembly (detachable interface to cleaning tool)
Working fluid reservoir and delivery system (nozzle, movable conduit)
Floor tool / nozzle attachment for vacuum cleaning appliance
rationale: Titles are dominated by 'cleaning appliance', 'floor tool', and 'vacuum cleaning', while abstracts repeatedly describe 'dental cleaning appliance' subsystems (handle, fluid reservoir, fluid delivery system, nozzle, moveable fluid conduit). Top TF-IDF terms ('floor', 'floor tool', 'suction', 'working fluid', 'reservoir', 'conduit', 'dental', 'handle') indicate two co-existing themes: dental fluid-jet cleaning and vacuum floor-cleaning tools. Evidence for modules is strongest for the dental fluid-delivery side; the floor/vacuum side is supported by title terms but no abstracts were provided, so confidence there is moderate.

suggested_cluster_label: Wearable air purification and air-treatment appliances
engineering_module_candidates: Motor-driven impeller / fan airflow generation module
Head-worn ear/speaker assembly (wearable form factor with audio)
Air purifier housing / air treatment apparatus
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('filter', 'air', 'airflow', 'fan', 'wearable', 'filter assembly', 'air purifier', 'hand dryer', 'drying') and representative titles like 'HEAD WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER' and 'AIR TREATMENT APPARATUS' indicate the cluster centers on air-moving appliances. Abstracts repeatedly cite a filter assembly, motor-driven impeller producing filtered airflow, and an ear/speaker assembly worn over the ears, directly supporting the wearable purifier and audio-integrated modules. The presence of 'dryer'/'hand dryer' and 'drying' terms suggests related airflow-based drying products are also included, though they are a smaller signal than the wearable purifier evidence (moderate confidence for the dryer module).
